description_long: This object is a stringed musical instrument with a circular wooden body and a long, slender neck. The body features a drum-like resonator with a taut, light-colored membrane, encircled by metallic brackets. The neck is fitted with evenly spaced frets and is attached to a headstock with four visible tuning pegs, arranged in a two-per-side configuration. The strings run from the tailpiece across the membrane, over a bridge, and up the neck to the headstock. The wood is a warm brown, contrasting with the metallic and light membrane elements. The overall design is reminiscent of a traditional banjo, commonly seen in street performances or urban music gatherings.
description: A wooden stringed instrument with a circular resonator body, metallic brackets, and a long neck with four tuning pegs, resembling a banjo.
description_view_0: Side view showing the depth of the circular body, the neck, and the tuning pegs at the headstock.
description_view_1: Angled top-down view highlighting the round resonator, membrane surface, and the full length of the neck.
description_view_2: Opposite side view emphasizing the body’s thickness, metallic brackets, and the neck’s profile.
description_view_3: Front view displaying the full face of the resonator, all strings, bridge, and the headstock with tuning pegs.
attribute_headstock_design: Rectangular headstock with four tuning pegs, two on each side, no visible logo.
